checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: Only 2 of 5 plan-required tests have non-skipped implementations (Test #5 is t.Skip, Tests #3 and #4 delivered in s0, #1 and #2 in s1). Even the 4 non-skipped tests have gaps: TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA omits Grade=='N/A' and Unsupported==true assertions; TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected uses 'go' (supported) instead of TierGeneric as plan mandates; TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks omits grade (A/B/C/D/F) assertion; TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored relies on TierGeneric nil shortcut rather than specifically asserting maintainability != 0.95.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Only scorer_test.go was modified, matching the plan's single target file; no production code, language_tiers.go, or other files were touched.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected uses 'go' (supported) instead of TierGeneric to avoid triggering the unsupported path where 0.5 defaults could reappear — this weakens the guard. TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA omits Grade/Unsupported assertions the plan explicitly required. TestScore_AggregateSkipsUnsupported is t.Skip'd (honest but unimplemented). TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored passes via TierGeneric nil-return without proving maintainability != 0.95.
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: No security risk, but the DefaultsNotInjected test using 'go' instead of TierGeneric means the old-0.5-default-injection guard for unsupported languages is not actually tested, leaving a regression gap. SupportedLanguage_StillWorks lacks a grade assertion, reducing its value as a regression guard. Advancing would ship under-tested coverage.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence

dimensions.evidence: Only 2 of 5 plan-required tests have non-skipped implementations (Test #5 is t.Skip, Tests #3 and #4 delivered in s0, #1 and #2 in s1). Even the 4 non-skipped tests have gaps: TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA omits Grade=='N/A' and Unsupported==true assertions; TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected uses 'go' (supported) instead of TierGeneric as plan mandates; TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks omits grade (A/B/C/D/F) assertion; TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored relies on TierGeneric nil shortcut rather than specifically asserting maintainability != 0.95.
Only scorer_test.go was modified, matching the plan's single target file; no production code, language_tiers.go, or other files were touched.

TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected uses 'go' (supported) instead of TierGeneric to avoid triggering the unsupported path where 0.5 defaults could reappear — this weakens the guard. TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA omits Grade/Unsupported assertions the plan explicitly required. TestScore_AggregateSkipsUnsupported is t.Skip'd (honest but unimplemented). TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored passes via TierGeneric nil-return without proving maintainability != 0.95.

No security risk, but the DefaultsNotInjected test using 'go' instead of TierGeneric means the old-0.5-default-injection guard for unsupported languages is not actually tested, leaving a regression gap. SupportedLanguage_StillWorks lacks a grade assertion, reducing its value as a regression guard. Advancing would ship under-tested coverage.

failureReasons: Only 2 of 5 plan-required test functions have non-skipped implementations (AggregateSkipsUnsupported is t.Skip, the other 4 have gaps)
TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA omits required Grade=='N/A' and Unsupported==true assertions per plan
TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected uses 'go' (supported language) instead of TierGeneric as the plan explicitly requires, weakening the guard
TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks omits grade (A/B/C/D/F) assertion required by plan
TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored relies on TierGeneric nil-return shortcut rather than specifically asserting maintainability != 0.95
